---
title: "Checking Status"
---

Check the current state of a DAG run, including per-step breakdown and error details.

## Latest run

```bash
daggle status my-dag
```

Shows the most recent run with overall status, start/end time, duration, and a table of steps with their individual statuses.

## Specific run

```bash
daggle status my-dag --run-id 20260401T093012
```

## Status values

| Status | Meaning |
|-----------|----------------------------------------------|
| running | Step is currently executing |
| completed | Step finished successfully (exit code 0) |
| failed | Step exited with a non-zero code |
| waiting | Step is queued, waiting on dependencies |
| cancelled | Run was cancelled before the step could start |
| skipped | Step was skipped due to a `when:` condition |
| cached | Step was skipped because its inputs have not changed since the last successful run (requires `cache: true`) |

## Error messages

When a step fails, daggle extracts the R error message from stderr and shows it inline:

```
Step: validate
Status: failed
Duration: 2.3s
Error: Error in check_schema(df) : column "revenue" not found
```

This saves you from digging through log files for common failures.

## Step outputs

If steps produce [outputs](../writing-dags/data-passing.qmd), the status display includes them:

```
Step: train_model
Status: completed
Outputs:
  model_path: /data/models/rf_20260401.rds
  accuracy: 0.94
```

## Annotations

Any notes attached to a run with `daggle annotate` surface in the status output:

```
Annotations:
  [2026-04-17 08:45:12] alice: DB was down — manual restart at 08:30
```

See `daggle annotate` in the [CLI reference](../reference/cli.qmd#daggle-annotate) for how to write them.

## Related diagnostics

- [`daggle why`](../reference/cli.qmd#daggle-why) — focused failure diagnostic that combines status, stderr tail, and DAG-hash drift on one screen.
- [`daggle monitor`](../reference/cli.qmd#daggle-monitor) — interactive TUI that streams events live while the run is in progress.
